_note: PLACEHOLDER — replace with the real book.json from the original Spiritual-Seasons-PWA repo. The schema below is correct; the content is sample data only.
title: Spiritual Seasons
subtitle: A 120-Day Personal Devotional Workbook
author: Dr. Jacqueline Ghee, MSW, MPCC, DMIN
description: A journey through four spiritual seasons — Winter, Spring, Summer, and Autumn — guided by daily scripture and reflection.
acknowledgements: Placeholder — replace with Dr. Ghee's acknowledgements.
aboutAuthor: Placeholder — replace with Dr. Ghee's bio.

introduction.text: Welcome to Spiritual Seasons. This devotional is designed to meet you wherever you are on your spiritual journey.
introduction.scripture: There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens. — Ecclesiastes 3:1

steps: Take the quiz to discover your current spiritual season.
Read the daily scripture and let it settle in your heart.
Reflect on the prompt and write your journal entry.
Mark the day complete when you are ready.
Return tomorrow — one day at a time.

days.scriptureText: Be still, and know that I am God.
days.prompt: Where in your life are you straining to be in control? What would it look like to release that to God today?

days.scriptureText: But those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength.
days.prompt: In what area of your life are you most weary? How might waiting on God renew you?

days.scriptureText: His mercies are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.
days.prompt: What mercy do you need fresh from God today?

days.scriptureText: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want. He makes me lie down in green pastures.
days.prompt: What does rest look like for you right now? Are you resisting it?

days.scriptureText: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
days.prompt: What burden are you carrying that you have not yet brought to Jesus?

days.scriptureText: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding.
days.prompt: Where are you leaning on your own understanding instead of trusting God?

days.scriptureText: And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him.
days.prompt: What difficult circumstance are you having trouble believing God can work for good?

days.scriptureText: For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord, plans to prosper you.
days.prompt: Do you trust God's plan for your life right now? Where does doubt creep in?

days.scriptureText: Truly my soul finds rest in God; my salvation comes from him.
days.prompt: What does soul-rest mean to you, and how close are you to it today?

days.scriptureText: Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
days.prompt: What anxiety have you been holding onto that you can cast on God today?

days.scriptureText: Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.
days.prompt: Write out a specific worry as a prayer of thanksgiving.

days.scriptureText: So do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God.
days.prompt: What fear is most active in your life today? Write a response to it from Isaiah 41:10.

days.scriptureText: Wait for the Lord; be strong and take heart and wait for the Lord.
days.prompt: What are you waiting on God for? What does it feel like to wait?

days.scriptureText: We also glory in our sufferings, because we know that suffering produces perseverance.
days.prompt: What has a difficult season taught you about perseverance?

days.scriptureText: My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.
days.prompt: Where are you most aware of your weakness today? How might God's power show up there?

days.scriptureText: The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
days.prompt: Where is your heart broken right now? Write honestly about it.

days.scriptureText: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. Not as the world gives.
days.prompt: What is the difference between God's peace and the world's peace in your experience?

days.scriptureText: God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in trouble.
days.prompt: What trouble are you facing? How have you experienced God as refuge?

days.scriptureText: Let us then approach God's throne of grace with confidence.
days.prompt: Is there anything blocking you from approaching God with confidence? Write about it.

days.scriptureText: Whoever dwells in the shelter of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
days.prompt: What would it mean for you to dwell — not just visit — in God's presence?

days.scriptureText: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you.
days.prompt: Describe a 'river' you are passing through. What does God's presence look like in it?

days.scriptureText: Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged.
days.prompt: What step of courage is God inviting you to take?

days.scriptureText: I keep my eyes always on the Lord. With him at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
days.prompt: What are you keeping your eyes on instead of the Lord? How does that feel different?

days.scriptureText: For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid, but gives us power, love and self-discipline.
days.prompt: Where does timidity show up in your life? How might the Spirit's power change that?

days.scriptureText: The Lord is good, a refuge in times of trouble. He cares for those who trust in him.
days.prompt: Do you believe God is good even in this season? Write honestly.

days.scriptureText: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him.
days.prompt: How much hope do you feel right now? Ask God to fill you with it.

days.scriptureText: Cast your cares on the Lord and he will sustain you.
days.prompt: Name three specific cares you are casting on the Lord today.

days.scriptureText: You will keep in perfect peace those whose minds are steadfast, because they trust in you.
days.prompt: What would a steadfast mind look like for you? What would need to change?

days.scriptureText: I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
days.prompt: Are you experiencing the full life Jesus promised? What is missing?

days.scriptureText: He who was seated on the throne said, 'I am making everything new!'
days.prompt: As your Winter season closes, what has God been making new in you?

days.scriptureText: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: the old has gone, the new is here!
days.prompt: What old thing is passing away in your life? What new thing is emerging?

days.scriptureText: Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
days.prompt: What good thing are you tempted to give up on?

days.scriptureText: That person is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season.
days.prompt: What are you planted in right now — anxiety, distraction, or the Word?

days.scriptureText: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much fruit.
days.prompt: How connected to the vine do you feel today? What is draining that connection?

days.scriptureText: I can do all this through him who gives me strength.
days.prompt: What challenge requires more strength than you have in yourself?

days.scriptureText: Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind.
days.prompt: What pattern of the world are you conforming to that needs transformation?

days.scriptureText: Put off your old self and put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
days.prompt: What is one part of your old self you are actively putting off?

days.scriptureText: Let us examine our ways and test them, and let us return to the Lord.
days.prompt: Take a few minutes to examine your ways. What do you find?

days.scriptureText: Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
days.prompt: What does a pure heart look like in your specific circumstances today?

days.scriptureText: I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you.
days.prompt: What hardness in your heart is God working to soften?

days.scriptureText: But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
days.prompt: What are you seeking first right now — honestly?

days.scriptureText: Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.
days.prompt: What earthly thing is dominating your mind this week?

days.scriptureText: Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds.
days.prompt: What trial are you facing? How might you find joy in it?

days.scriptureText: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins.
days.prompt: Is there something you need to confess? Write it out and receive forgiveness.

days.scriptureText: Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
days.prompt: What are you allowing into your heart unchecked?

days.scriptureText: We were therefore buried with him through baptism into death in order that we might live a new life.
days.prompt: What does living a new life look like practically for you this week?

days.scriptureText: I have hidden your word in my heart that I might not sin against you.
days.prompt: What scripture are you hiding in your heart right now? Write it out.

days.scriptureText: Let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles.
days.prompt: What weight is hindering you right now? Name it specifically.

days.scriptureText: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal.
days.prompt: What past thing are you struggling to forget or let go?

days.scriptureText: He saved us through the washing of rebirth and renewing of the Holy Spirit.
days.prompt: How have you experienced the renewing work of the Holy Spirit?

days.scriptureText: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
days.prompt: What new beginning is God creating in your life?

days.scriptureText: See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it?
days.prompt: Do you see what God is doing? Or are you missing it? Write about that.

days.scriptureText: No one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again.
days.prompt: Reflect on your new birth. What changed? What is still changing?

days.scriptureText: And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you...
days.prompt: The resurrection power of God lives in you. Where do you need it most today?

days.scriptureText: Blessed is the one who trusts in the Lord. They will be like a tree planted by the water.
days.prompt: Are your roots growing deeper this season? What is feeding them?

days.scriptureText: The righteous will flourish like a palm tree.
days.prompt: What would flourishing look like in your life right now?

days.scriptureText: See! The winter is past; the rains are over and gone. Flowers appear on the earth.
days.prompt: What sign of new life do you see emerging in your spiritual journey?

days.scriptureText: Repent, then, and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped out, that times of refreshing may come from the Lord.
days.prompt: What act of turning to God is refreshing you this season?

days.scriptureText: Do not despise these small beginnings, for the Lord rejoices to see the work begin.
days.prompt: What small beginning is God calling you not to despise?

days.scriptureText: Those who sow with tears will reap with songs of joy.
days.prompt: As Spring closes, what seeds have you planted in tears that you are trusting God to harvest?

days.scriptureText: I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
days.prompt: What does a full life look like for you right now? What is blocking it?

days.scriptureText: The joy of the Lord is your strength.
days.prompt: Is your joy in God your actual source of strength today, or are you drawing from another well?

days.scriptureText: You make known to me the path of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence.
days.prompt: When were you last filled with joy in God's presence? Describe it.

days.scriptureText: Every good and perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of the heavenly lights.
days.prompt: List five good gifts in your life today. Spend time in gratitude for each one.

days.scriptureText: This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
days.prompt: What is one specific thing about today you can rejoice in?

days.scriptureText: I have learned, in whatever state I am, to be content.
days.prompt: What makes contentment difficult for you right now?

days.scriptureText: Rejoice always, pray continually, give thanks in all circumstances.
days.prompt: Give thanks in your current circumstances. Write out your prayer of gratitude.

days.scriptureText: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies.
days.prompt: Where has God provided for you even in a hard situation?

days.scriptureText: Now to him who is able to do immeasurably more than all we ask or imagine.
days.prompt: Where are you limiting what you expect from God?

days.scriptureText: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.
days.prompt: In what area of your life do you need to walk in a conquering spirit?

days.scriptureText: Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise.
days.prompt: How do you typically enter into God's presence? With thanksgiving or with requests?

days.scriptureText: Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over.
days.prompt: Where is God calling you to give generously right now?

days.scriptureText: All these blessings will come on you and accompany you if you obey the Lord your God.
days.prompt: What blessing has followed your obedience? Trace the connection.

days.scriptureText: Take delight in the Lord, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
days.prompt: What are the desires of your heart? Are they aligned with delighting in God?

days.scriptureText: The Lord bless you and keep you; the Lord make his face shine on you.
days.prompt: Write a blessing over someone in your life using these words as inspiration.

days.scriptureText: The Lord will guide you always; he will satisfy your needs in a sun-scorched land.
days.prompt: Where are you in a 'sun-scorched land' right now? How is God satisfying you there?

days.scriptureText: Whoever believes in me, as Scripture has said, rivers of living water will flow from within them.
days.prompt: Is living water flowing from you or are you feeling spiritually dry? What does that tell you?

days.scriptureText: And God is able to bless you abundantly, so that in all things at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.
days.prompt: Where have you experienced God's abundant blessing recently?

days.scriptureText: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
days.prompt: What helps you see God most clearly? What blurs that vision?

days.scriptureText: For the Lord God is a sun and shield; the Lord bestows favor and honor.
days.prompt: Write about a time God's favor was unmistakably evident in your life.

days.scriptureText: Yet I will rejoice in the Lord, I will be joyful in God my Savior.
days.prompt: Can you choose joy today regardless of circumstances? Write your declaration.

days.scriptureText: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence.
days.prompt: Where do you experience the fullness of joy most consistently?

days.scriptureText: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever.
days.prompt: Write a list of evidence that God's love for you endures.

days.scriptureText: They will celebrate your abundant goodness and joyfully sing of your righteousness.
days.prompt: Write your own celebration of God's goodness.

days.scriptureText: Dear friend, I pray that you may enjoy good health and that all may go well with you, even as your soul is getting along well.
days.prompt: How is your soul getting along right now? Be honest.

days.scriptureText: Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the one who takes refuge in him.
days.prompt: What is your most recent experience of tasting God's goodness?

days.scriptureText: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control.
days.prompt: Which fruit of the Spirit is most evident in your life? Which is most absent?

days.scriptureText: A cheerful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
days.prompt: How is the state of your heart affecting your health — physically and spiritually?

days.scriptureText: The Lord your God is with you, the Mighty Warrior who saves. He will take great delight in you.
days.prompt: Sit with the truth that God delights in you. What comes up?

days.scriptureText: You turned my wailing into dancing; you removed my sackcloth and clothed me with joy.
days.prompt: As Summer closes, where has God turned mourning to dancing in your story?

days.scriptureText: There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens.
days.prompt: What season of life are you in? What is its unique invitation?

days.scriptureText: Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
days.prompt: If today were your last, what would matter most?

days.scriptureText: Very truly I tell you, unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds.
days.prompt: What must die in you to produce greater life for others?

days.scriptureText: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
days.prompt: Where are you most aware of your poverty of spirit? How does God meet you there?

days.scriptureText: All people are like grass, and all their glory is like the flowers of the field; the grass withers and the flowers fall.
days.prompt: What are you clinging to that is temporary? What is eternal?

days.scriptureText: Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.
days.prompt: What does taking up your cross look like in your daily life?

days.scriptureText: Offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God.
days.prompt: What would being a living sacrifice look like for you today?

days.scriptureText: Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy, where thieves break in and steal.
days.prompt: What earthly treasure are you storing up at the expense of heavenly treasure?

days.scriptureText: Show me, Lord, my life's end and the number of my days; let me know how fleeting my life is.
days.prompt: How does the fleeting nature of life change how you want to live today?

days.scriptureText: For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal glory that far outweighs them all.
days.prompt: What trouble feels heavy right now? Write it in light of eternity.

days.scriptureText: Now faith is confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we do not see.
days.prompt: What are you trusting God for that you cannot yet see?

days.scriptureText: Even when I am old and gray, do not forsake me, my God, till I declare your power to the next generation.
days.prompt: What of God's power do you want to pass on?

days.scriptureText: Even to your old age and gray hairs I am he, I am he who will sustain you.
days.prompt: How have you seen God sustain you through change and loss?

days.scriptureText: He who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus.
days.prompt: What unfinished work is God doing in you that requires patience?

days.scriptureText: What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.
days.prompt: What do you want the mist of your life to leave behind?

days.scriptureText: He is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
days.prompt: What feels like it is falling apart? Write a prayer trusting God to hold it.

days.scriptureText: I consider that our present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed in us.
days.prompt: What current suffering are you comparing to what God promises?

days.scriptureText: Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.
days.prompt: What valley are you walking through? Write about God's presence in it.

days.scriptureText: He will swallow up death forever. The Sovereign Lord will wipe away the tears from all faces.
days.prompt: What loss or grief are you carrying? Write a lament and then a hope.

days.scriptureText: Jesus said to her, 'I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die.'
days.prompt: How does the resurrection change how you face loss and endings?

days.scriptureText: Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his faithful servants.
days.prompt: What does it mean to you that your life — and your death — is precious to God?

days.scriptureText: He will wipe every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death or mourning or crying or pain.
days.prompt: What pain do you most long to see God make right?

days.scriptureText: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
days.prompt: What would it look like to finish well? What might threaten that?

days.scriptureText: Your labor in the Lord is not in vain.
days.prompt: What work feels futile right now? How does this verse reframe it?

days.scriptureText: Well done, good and faithful servant!
days.prompt: What would it mean to hear these words? What faithfulness is God asking of you now?

days.scriptureText: Praise the Lord, my soul, and forget not all his benefits.
days.prompt: Write a list of God's benefits in your life that you are at risk of forgetting.

days.scriptureText: Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the Lord your God goes with you.
days.prompt: What ending or transition in your life calls for courage right now?

days.scriptureText: But I trust in you, Lord; I say, 'You are my God.' My times are in your hands.
days.prompt: What does it mean that your times — your seasons — are in God's hands?

days.scriptureText: Fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith.
days.prompt: As you near the end of your journey, what has fixing your eyes on Jesus changed?

days.scriptureText: I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.
days.prompt: You have completed 120 days. What has God begun and completed in you through this journey?

winter.title: Winter — Stillness & Trust
winter.description: Winter is a season of rest, waiting, and deep trust. The landscape appears barren, but roots are growing unseen. This is the season of learning to be still.
winter.scripture: Be still, and know that I am God. — Psalm 46:10

spring.title: Spring — Renewal & Planting
spring.description: Spring is a season of new beginnings, fresh starts, and planting seeds of faith. Things that appeared dead are coming to life. This is the season of hope and renewal.
spring.scripture: See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? — Isaiah 43:19

summer.title: Summer — Abundance & Joy
summer.description: Summer is a season of flourishing, gratitude, and overflowing joy. Seeds planted in faith are bearing fruit. This is the season of celebrating God's goodness.
summer.scripture: The joy of the Lord is your strength. — Nehemiah 8:10

autumn.title: Autumn — Harvest & Letting Go
autumn.description: Autumn is a season of harvest, release, and holy preparation. What is finished must be released to make room for what is coming. This is the season of wisdom and surrender.
autumn.scripture: There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens. — Ecclesiastes 3:1
